Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

I imagine it was lu-2580 that you wanted to file against.
Which I think we fixed by actually ensuring fsync works (And coreutils cp does fsync on the source file first)

Also I think your fix greatly slows down a very comon file of "create file, copy file" on a single node because where as before copy will read file from local cache, your fiemap change will now force cache drop and reread from the server which is very undesirable.
Patch Set 2: (5 inline comments)

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(22 inline comments)

Thanks for the patch, I think this is fixing an important issue, even if it might be worked around in the current master code.

Several style cleanups are needed that should have been caught by the checkpatch.pl script run at commit time.

The more major issue is the performance impact of "write; cp" on a single client, which I think could be addressed fairly cleanly.

Other major issue is that the sanityn.sh test case is not actually running the test program.

Ran 18 tests.  10 tests failed: sanity-hsm, runtests, lustre-rsync-test, sanity-quota, insanity, replay-ost-single, recovery-small, conf-sanity, sanityn, sanity.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I like this patch a lot better, but it still suffers from the same problem that it will always revoke the locks from the calling client, because there is no data beyond [i_size, EOF]. It would also always cause a lock revocation for sparse files.

Since the calling client has flushed it's cache already due to FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC, it should be possible to have the OST skip locking on extents that are locked by the calling client?  That would avoid any lock contention on the common "calling client holds [0, EOF] lock" case, but still handle the distributed writers/FIEMAP case correctly. 

(style) in sanity.sh there is no need for linefeed escape '\' after "||"

Patch Set 5:

Niu Yawei, thanks for update. I have developed exact same patch (OBD_FL_SRVLOCK condition set) you uploaded, but my patch have some unresolved issues. But there are some places that I copied from my patch here:
1) test, that check extra lock is not held then same client do copy and fiemap
2) checking OBD_FL_SRVLOCK
3) fiemap does not necessary get info for the whole file, but rather for region specified in fiemap.fm_start & fm_length
4) ost_lock_get takes start & count, not start & end

Patch Set 9:

I'm a little worried that Patch 8 didn't pass on zfs for the new test 71.  I would like to see it pass review-zfs before it lands.

Patch Set 9: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Nathaniel, you're right - FIEMAP is not supported on ZFS, so this test should be skipped:

        dd if=/dev/urandom of=$DIR1/$tfile bs=40K seek=1 count=1
        [ "$(facet_fstype ost$(($($GETSTRIPE -i $DIR1/$tfile) + 1)))" = "zfs" ] &&
                skip "ORI-366/LU-1941: FIEMAP unimplemented on ZFS" && return 0

parents.subject: LU-3264 tests: support failover testing with ZFS

commit.subject: LU-3219 ost: Ensure dirty flushed on fiemap ioctl
commit.message:
LU-3219 ost: Ensure dirty flushed on fiemap ioctl

Data corruption is possible if cp(coreutils) uses FIEMAP to obtain
data holes in shared file, since there could be dirty cache on
other clients which hasn't been flushed back.

To ensure all the dirty on remote clients being flushed back on
fiemap ioctl, we'd acquire ldlm lock on server side for fiemap,
unless the local client (which invoke fiemap) has cached lock.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-1001
Signed-off-by: Artem Blagodarenko <artem_blagodarenko@xyratex.com>
Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I67fe05bac1ce75f98c3d3806a4d08f6cb02048e6
